mission.title: Install and Configure Kuadrant on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuadrant is an operator that manages the lifecycle of Kuadrant components, enhancing gateway providers with features like TLS, DNS, application authentication, and rate limiting. Installing Kuadrant allows you to leverage these capabilities in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Kuadrant Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Kuadrant Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kuadrant https://kuadrant.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Kuadrant Operator
steps.description:
Install the Kuadrant Operator in a new namespace called 'kuadrant' using the following command:
```bash
helm install kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant --create-namespace --version v1.4.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kuadrant Operator pod is running with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kuadrant
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
To configure resource limits for the Kuadrant Operator, you can edit the deployment with the following command:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant
```
Add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Kuadrant Operator running in the 'kuadrant' namespace, with its pod status showing as 'Running'. You can further configure it as needed.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kuadrant https://kuadrant.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update
helm install kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant --create-namespace --version v1.4.1

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kuadrant Operator release using Helm with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by the Kuadrant Operator with the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete crd kuadrant*.kuadrant.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kuadrant' namespace and verify its removal with the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kuadrant
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Kuadrant Operator with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kuadrant -o yaml > kuadrant-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Kuadrant Operator to the latest version with the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant --version v1.5.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Kuadrant Operator pod is running the new version with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kuadrant
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kuadrant Operator pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors with the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kuadrant
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, check the installed version with:
```bash
helm list --namespace kuadrant
```
Then ensure the version you are upgrading to is compatible.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found after upgrade
troubleshooting.description:
If the CRDs are not found after an upgrade, ensure they are still installed with:
```bash
kubectl get crd --namespace kuadrant
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If the resource limits are not applied as expected, check the deployment configuration with:
```bash
kubectl get deployment kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant -o yaml
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
